message:
feat(workflows)(ft-3p7re): add overall workflow deadline (workflow_total_deadline_ms)

Pre-fix: WorkflowRunnerConfig only enforced step_timeout_ms (per-step
default 30s). A pathologically retrying workflow with bounded step
duration but unbounded total step count could pin a pane forever —
ft-3p7re's audit flagged this as the runaway-prevention gap.

Fix:
- WorkflowRunnerConfig gains `workflow_total_deadline_ms: u64`.
  Default 600_000 (10 minutes) — generous for legitimate workflows,
  short enough to bound runaway pathology. Set to 0 to disable
  (legacy behavior preserved as opt-out).
- New per-step deadline seam in run_workflow_inner mirrors the
  existing cx-cancel cleanup pattern: when elapsed >= deadline,
  run fail_execution + mark_trigger_event_handled("error") +
  lock_manager.release + record_workflow_terminal_action, then
  return WorkflowExecutionResult::Error with a structured reason
  string that names elapsed_ms and the deadline. Both cx-aware
  (with_cx) and legacy (None-cx) caller paths get the same
  cleanup discipline.
- Reason format: "run_workflow exceeded overall deadline at step
  {N}: elapsed {M}ms >= {D}ms (workflow_total_deadline_ms)".

Test coverage:
- runner_config_defaults pinned at 600_000.
- runner_config_custom_values verifies struct-literal construction
  with a 30-min override.
- new runner_config_disabled_overall_deadline_is_zero pins the
  opt-out sentinel.
- workflow_runner_config_defaults_under_labruntime extended to
  cover the new field under deterministic LabRuntime.

Wiring covers all 3 existing WorkflowRunnerConfig construction
sites: the Default impl, two struct literals in runner.rs tests,
and the `..Default::default()`-using construction in
workflows/mod.rs:6233 picks up the new field automatically.

Lib compile-checks clean.

Bead: ft-3p7re (P2 codebase-audit, runaway-workflow prevention).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
